task: Check 3 key pages (landing, explore, tool detail) for accessibility: missing alt text, low contrast text, touch targets under 44px, missing ARIA labels on interactive elements. Fix anything clear-cut.

assignments.frontend: Audit and fix accessibility issues across 3 route files: `src/indiestack/routes/landing.py`, `src/indiestack/routes/explore.py`, and `src/indiestack/routes/tool.py`, plus shared components in `src/indiestack/routes/components.py`. Specifically check for and fix: (1) missing alt text on all tags, (2) low contrast text — any text using light grays like #999, #aaa, #bbb on white/light backgrounds, (3) touch targets under 44px on buttons, links, and interactive elements (check for min-height/min-width/padding), (4) missing ARIA labels on interactive elements like buttons, icon-only links, form inputs, toggles, and search fields. Also check for missing form

decomposition.reasoning: This is a pure frontend/HTML accessibility audit. All three pages are f-string HTML templates in route files, and shared components live in components.py. A single frontend pass can audit all files and apply fixes. No backend logic, deployment, content changes, or MCP work needed — just HTML attribute and CSS fixes.

strategy_review.reasoning: Well-scoped, low-risk hygiene task. Accessibility fixes (alt text, ARIA labels) directly improve SEO — which matters more than the accessibility itself at 53 users. Alt text on images is literally free SEO. Touch target fixes improve mobile UX, which is where most dev browsing happens. The scope is tight (4 files, specific checklist), the instruction explicitly says 'don't redesign', and past frontend tasks in this complexity range ran $1-2. Worth the spend.

approved_tasks.frontend: Audit and fix accessibility issues across 3 route files: `src/indiestack/routes/landing.py`, `src/indiestack/routes/explore.py`, and `src/indiestack/routes/tool.py`, plus shared components in `src/indiestack/routes/components.py`. Specifically check for and fix: (1) missing alt text on all tags, (2) low contrast text — any text using light grays like #999, #aaa, #bbb on white/light backgrounds, (3) touch targets under 44px on buttons, links, and interactive elements (check for min-height/min-width/padding), (4) missing ARIA labels on interactive elements like buttons, icon-only links, form inputs, toggles, and search fields. Also check for missing form

conditions: Run smoke tests after changes — components.py is shared across ALL pages, not just the 3 being audited

For contrast changes: only fix genuinely unreadable text (e.g. #999 on white). Don't touch intentional muted/secondary text styling that's part of the design hierarchy — changing #888 to #555 everywhere would flatten the visual system
Do not change any colors that are defined as CSS variables in :root — if a variable value needs changing, flag it but don't change it, since it cascades everywhere
risk_flags: components.py changes (especially touch target padding) cascade to every page — visual regression risk beyond the 3 pages being audited
Bumping touch targets with padding can break tight layouts, especially on mobile grid/flex containers
